channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Payload Ransomware claims the hack of Royal Bahrain Hospital
item.description: The Payload Ransomware group claims to have breached the Royal Bahrain Hospital (RBH), a leading healthcare facility in Bahrain. The Payload Ransomware group claims to have hacked the Royal Bahrain Hospital (RBH) and...

item.title: Security Affairs newsletter Round 567 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
item.description: A new round of the weekly Security Affairs newsletter has arrived! Every week, the best security articles from Security Affairs are free in your email box. Enjoy a new round of the weekly SecurityAffairs newsletter, i...

item.title: Loblaw Data Breach Impacts Customer Information
item.description: Personal information such as names, email addresses, and phone numbers was accessed by hackers. The post Loblaw Data Breach Impacts Customer Information appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Week in review: AiTM phishing kit used to hijack AWS accounts, year-long malware campaign targets HR
item.description: Here’s an overview of some of last week’s most interesting news, articles, interviews and videos: Turning expertise into opportunity for women in cybersecurity Speaker diversity in cybersecurity has been a talking poi...

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 11 OOB hotpatch to fix RRAS RCE flaw
item.description: Microsoft has released an out-of-band (OOB) update to fix a security vulnerabilities affecting Windows 11 Enterprise devices that receive hotpatch updates instead of the regular Patch Tuesday cumulative updates. [...]

item.title: Deploy AWS applications and access AWS accounts across multiple Regions with IAM Identity Center
item.description: If your organization relies on AWS IAM Identity Center for workforce access, you can now extend that access across multiple AWS Regions with multi-Region replication. Previously, AWS access portal was only available i...

item.title: Starbucks data breach impacts 889 employees
item.description: Starbucks disclosed a breach after phishing attacks on its employee portal led to unauthorized access to Partner Central accounts, exposing staff data. Starbucks reported a data breach affecting hundreds of employees...

item.title: OpenClaw AI Agent Flaws Could Enable Prompt Injection and Data Exfiltration
item.description: China's National Computer Network Emergency Response Technical Team (CNCERT) has issued a warning about the security stemming from the use of OpenClaw (formerly Clawdbot and Moltbot), an open-source and self-hosted au...

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.description: This is a current list of where and when I am scheduled to speak: I’m giving the Ross Anderson Lecture at the University of Cambridge’s Churchill College at 5:30 PM GMT on Thursday, March 19, 2026. I’m speaking at RSA...

item.title: GlassWorm Supply-Chain Attack Abuses 72 Open VSX Extensions to Target Developers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new iteration of the GlassWorm campaign that they say represents a "significant escalation" in how it propagates through the Open VSX registry. "Instead of requiring every mali...

item.title: Storm-2561 lures victims to spoofed VPN sites to harvest corporate logins
item.description: Attackers linked to Storm-2561 use SEO-poisoned search results to lure users to fake Ivanti, Cisco, and Fortinet VPN sites that steal corporate login credentials. In mid-January 2026, Microsoft Defender Experts uncove...

item.title: Critical HPE AOS-CX Vulnerability Allows Admin Password Resets
item.description: The vulnerability can be exploited remotely, without authentication, to circumvent existing authentication controls. The post Critical HPE AOS-CX Vulnerability Allows Admin Password Resets appeared first on SecurityWe...

item.title: Interpol – Operation Synergia III leads to 45,000 malicious IPs dismantled and 94 arrests worldwide
item.description: INTERPOL dismantled 45,000 malicious IPs and servers and arrested 94 suspects in a global cybercrime operation. INTERPOL announced a global cybercrime operation (codenamed Operation Synergia III) involving 72 countrie...

item.title: Microsoft: Windows 11 users can't access C: drive on some Samsung PCs
item.description: Microsoft is investigating a new issue affecting some Samsung laptops running Windows 11 after installing the February 2026 security updates, in which users lose access to their C:\ drive and are unable to launch appl...

item.title: U.S. CISA adds Google Chrome flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds Google Chrome flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added two Google C...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Increased Squid Population in the Falklands
item.description: Some good news : squid stocks seem to be recovering in the waters off the Falkland Islands. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog modera...

item.title: FBI seeks victims of Steam games used to spread malware
item.description: The FBI is asking gamers who installed Steam titles containing malware to provide information as part of an ongoing investigation into eight malicious games uploaded to the gaming platform. [...]

item.title: Hackers targeted Poland’s National Centre for Nuclear Research
item.description: Hackers targeted Poland’s National Centre for Nuclear Research, but security systems detected and blocked the attack before any damage. The National Centre for Nuclear Research in Poland reported a cyberattack on its...

item.title: Fake PoCs, Misunderstood Risks Cause Cisco SD-WAN Chaos
item.description: The excitement around Cisco's latest SD-WAN bugs has inspired some light fraud, misunderstandings, and overlooked risks.

item.title: The Data Gap: Why Nonprofit Cyber Incidents Go Underreported
item.description: Threat actors target nonprofits due to security gaps and highly coveted information, but a lack of sufficient data makes it difficult to grasp the entire picture.

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target Southeast Asian Militaries with AppleChris and MemFun Malware
item.description: A suspected China-based cyber espionage operation has targeted Southeast Asian military organizations as part of a state-sponsored campaign that dates back to at least 2020. Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 is tracking the...

item.title: Cyberattackers Don't Care About Good Causes
item.description: Sightline Security's founder and advisory board discuss how cybersecurity poses significant problems for nonprofits and suggest ways the industry can help.

item.title: Interpol's 'Operation Synergia III' Nets 94 Arrests in Major Cybercrime Sweep
item.description: A new law enforcement operation against phishing and ransomware operators led to the takedown of 45,000 malicious IP addresses

item.title: Will AI Save Consumers From Smartphone-Based Phishing Attacks?
item.description: Sophisticated phishing attacks are bypassing on-device protections with troubling frequency, making it more critical than ever for users to protect themselves from potential threats, new research from Omdia shows.

item.title: Starbucks Data Breach Impacts Employees
item.description: Starbucks said the incident involved phishing attacks targeting an employee portal, affecting hundreds. The post Starbucks Data Breach Impacts Employees appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: INTERPOL Dismantles 45,000 Malicious IPs, Arrests 94 in Global Cybercrime
item.description: INTERPOL on Friday announced the takedown of 45,000 malicious IP addresses and servers used in connection with phishing, malware, and ransomware campaigns, as part of the agency's ongoing efforts to dismantle criminal...

item.title: US and European authorities disrupt socksEscort proxy service tied to AVrecon botnet
item.description: Authorities in the US and Europe disrupted the SocksEscort proxy service, which used the AVrecon botnet and infected about 360,000 devices since 2020. Law enforcement agencies in the US and Europe have disrupted Socks...

item.title: In Other News: N8n Flaw Exploited, Slopoly Malware, Interpol Cybercrime Crackdown
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Telus Digital data breach, vulnerabilities in Linux AppArmor allow root privileges, US defense contractor behind Coruna exploits. The post In Other New...

item.title: Storm-2561 Spreads Trojan VPN Clients via SEO Poisoning to Steal Credentials
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a credential theft campaign that employs fake virtual private network (VPN) clients distributed through search engine optimization (SEO) poisoning techniques. "The campaign redirects...

item.title: Investigating a New Click-Fix Variant
item.description: Disclaimer: This report has been prepared by the Threat Research Center to enhance cybersecurity awareness and support the strengthening of defense capabilities. It is based on independent research and observations of...

item.title: Fake enterprise VPN sites used to steal company credentials
item.description: A threat actor tracked as Storm-2561 is distributing fake enterprise VPN clients from Ivanti, Cisco, and Fortinet to steal VPN credentials from unsuspecting users. [...]

item.title: Most Google Cloud Attacks Start With Bug Exploitation
item.description: Forget stolen credentials and misconfigurations; AI means vulnerability exploits that beat patching cycles are the top cause of compromises in the cloud.

item.title: Real-Time Banking Trojan Strikes Brazil's Pix Users
item.description: The latest banking Trojan campaign to hit Brazil combines classic malware with a real-time human operator, waiting for the perfect moment to strike.

item.title: Accertify’s Attack State targets credential stuffing and ATO attacks
item.description: Accertify has announced the launch of Attack State, a new capability in its Account Protection solution designed to help organizations detect and respond to coordinated login attacks and other automated threats target...

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Take Aim at US and Other Targets, Raising Risk of Cyberattacks During War
item.description: Pro-Iranian hackers are targeting sites in the Middle East and starting to stretch into the United States during the war, raising the risk of American defense contractors, power stations and water plants. The post Ira...

item.title: EU Parliament backs extension of CSAM detection rules until 2027
item.description: The European Parliament has voted to extend a temporary exemption to EU privacy legislation that allows online platforms to voluntarily detect child sexual abuse material (CSAM). The extension prolongs a derogation fr...

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-3909 Google Skia Out-of-Bounds Write Vulnerability CVE-2026-3910 Google...

item.title: Bold Security Emerges From Stealth With $40 Million in Funding
item.description: The startup relies on AI to turn devices into active agents that understand users’ actions and provide protection in real time. The post Bold Security Emerges From Stealth With $40 Million in Funding appeared first on...

item.title: AI-assisted Slopoly malware powers Hive0163’s ransomware campaigns
item.description: The Hive0163 group used AI-assisted malware called Slopoly to maintain persistent access in ransomware attacks. IBM X-Force researchers report that the financially motivated group Hive0163 is using AI-assisted malware...

item.title: Google Paid Out $17 Million in Bug Bounty Rewards in 2025
item.description: Google paid over $3.7 million for Chrome vulnerabilities, and more than $3.5 million for cloud security defects. The post Google Paid Out $17 Million in Bug Bounty Rewards in 2025 appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Academia and the “AI Brain Drain”
item.description: In 2025, Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Meta collectively spent US$380 billion on building artificial-intelligence tools. That number is expected to surge still higher this year, to $650 billion, to fund the building o...

item.title: Iran-Linked Hacker Attack on Stryker Disrupted Manufacturing and Shipping
item.description: Evidence indicates that the attackers leveraged existing endpoint management software rather than malware to wipe devices. The post Iran-Linked Hacker Attack on Stryker Disrupted Manufacturing and Shipping appeared fi...

item.title: Authorities dismantle SocksEscort proxy network behind millions in fraud
item.description: SocksEscort, a residential proxy network used to exploit thousands of compromised home routers worldwide and facilitate large-scale fraud that cost victims millions of dollars, has been disrupted in an international l...

item.title: Onyx Security Launches With $40 Million in Funding
item.description: The startup is building a control pane to help organizations oversee autonomous AI agents and rapidly adopt them. The post Onyx Security Launches With $40 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Google Fixes Two Chrome Zero-Days Exploited in the Wild Affecting Skia and V8
item.description: Google on Thursday released security updates for its Chrome web browser to address two high-severity vulnerabilities that it said have been exploited in the wild. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows - CVE-2026-3...

item.title: BioCatch DeviceIQ helps banks spot risky devices before login
item.description: BioCatch has announced the launch of DeviceIQ, a comprehensive new device identification and intelligence product that transforms how financial institutions evaluate the trustworthiness of devices used for digital ban...

item.title: Authorities Disrupt SocksEscort Proxy Service Powered by AVrecon Botnet
item.description: Law enforcement agencies in the US and Europe targeted the cybercrime service that has impacted 360,000 devices since 2020. The post Authorities Disrupt SocksEscort Proxy Service Powered by AVrecon Botnet appeared fir...

item.title: Nine CrackArmor Flaws in Linux AppArmor Enable Root Escalation, Bypass Container Isolation
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities within the Linux kernel's AppArmor module that could be exploited by unprivileged users to circumvent kernel protections, escalate to root, an...

item.title: Starbucks discloses data breach affecting hundreds of employees
item.description: Starbucks has disclosed a data breach affecting hundreds of employees after threat actors gained access to their Starbucks Partner Central accounts. [...]

item.title: A React-based phishing page with credential exfiltration via EmailJS, (Fri, Mar 13th)
item.description: On Wednesday, a phishing message made its way into our handler inbox that contained a fairly typical low-quality lure, but turned out to be quite interesting in the end nonetheless. That is because the accompanying cr...

item.title: Red Access firewall-native SSE adds GenAI security and browser protection to existing firewalls
item.description: Red Access has announced firewall-native SSE, an agentless cloud layer that instantly upgrades any existing firewall with Security Service Edge (SSE), GenAI security, and browser-agnostic protection. Deployed directly...

item.title: Google fixes two new Chrome zero-days exploited in attacks
item.description: Google has released emergency security updates to patch two high-severity Chrome vulnerabilities exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]

item.title: AI coding agents keep repeating decade-old security mistakes
item.description: Coding agents are now writing production features on real development teams, and a new report from DryRun Security shows that those agents introduce security vulnerabilities at a high rate across nearly every type of...

item.title: Passwords, MFA, and why neither is enough
item.description: Passwords weren’t enough, so we added MFA. Now MFA isn’t enough either. In this Help Net Security video, Karlo Zatylny, CTO/CISO at Portnox, walks through why each layer of identity security has failed and what comes...

item.title: Authorities Disrupt SocksEscort Proxy Botnet Exploiting 369,000 IPs Across 163 Countries
item.description: A court-authorized international law enforcement operation has dismantled a criminal proxy service named SocksEscort that enslaved thousands of residential routers worldwide into a botnet for committing large-scale fr...

item.title: New infosec products of the week: March 13, 2026
item.description: Here’s a look at the most interesting products from the past week, featuring releases from Binary Defense, Mend.io, OPSWAT, Singulr AI, SOC Prime, Terra Security, and Vicarius. Singulr AI’s Agent Pulse delivers enforc...

item.title: From legacy architecture to Cloudflare One
item.description: Learn how Cloudflare and CDW de-risk SASE migrations with a blueprint that treats legacy debt as an application modernization project.

item.title: Veeam Patches 7 Critical Backup & Replication Flaws Allowing Remote Code Execution
item.description: Veeam has released security updates to address multiple critical vulnerabilities in its Backup & Replication software that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution. The vulnerabilities are as...

item.title: Insights: Increased Risk of Wiper Attacks
item.description: We are observing an increase of wiper attacks by the Iran-linked Handala Hack group (aka Void Manticore) through phishing and misuse of Microsoft Intune. The post Insights: Increased Risk of Wiper Attacks appeared fir...

item.title: Canadian retail giant Loblaw notifies customers of data breach
item.description: Still, out of an abundance of caution, Loblaw says it has automatically logged out all customers from their accounts. Account holders who need to access the company's digital services will have to log in again. [...]

item.title: England Hockey investigating ransomware data breach
item.description: England Hockey, the governing body for field hockey in England, is investigating a potential data breach after the AiLock ransomware gang listed it as a victim on its data leak site. [...]

item.title: Rust-Based VENON Malware Targets 33 Brazilian Banks with Credential-Stealing Overlays
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new banking malware targeting Brazilian users that's written in Rust, marking a significant departure from other known Delphi-based malware families associated wit...

item.title: Delinea's StrongDM Acquisition Highlights the Changing Role of PAM
item.description: StrongDM, which injects ephemeral, real-time credentials into developer workflows, will enable Delinea to offer privilege access management across cloud, SaaS, Kubernetes, and database environments.

item.title: Hive0163 Uses AI-Assisted Slopoly Malware for Persistent Access in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a suspected artificial intelligence (AI)-generated malware codenamed Slopoly put to use by a financially motivated threat actor named Hive0163. "Although still relat...

item.title: How to manage the lifecycle of Amazon Machine Images using AMI Lineage for AWS
item.description: As organizations scale their cloud infrastructure, maintaining proper lifecycle management of Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) is a critical component of their security and risk management goals. AMIs provide the essentia...

item.title: PixRevolution Malware Hijacks Brazil's PIX Transfers in Real Time
item.description: PixRevolution Android trojan hijacks Brazil’s PIX payments in real time using accessibility abuse

item.title: Critical Zero-Click Flaw in n8n Allows Full Server Compromise
item.description: The critical vulnerability affecting both cloud and self-hosted n8n instances requires no authentication or even n8n account to be exploited

item.title: €1 million online fraud scheme uncovered, three suspects arrested
item.description: A criminal group suspected of running an online fraud scheme in Germany, which defrauded victims of around €1 million, has been dismantled through judicial cooperation coordinated by Eurojust. On 10 March, German and...

item.title: What Orgs Can Learn From Olympics, World Cup IR Plans
item.description: In this edition of "Reporters' Notebook," we discuss cyberattackers targeting the Milan-Cortina Winter Games, adding them to a long list of global sporting events in the crosshairs. Though the attack surface is grande...

item.title: How to Scale Phishing Detection in Your SOC: 3 Steps for CISOs
item.description: Phishing has quietly turned into one of the hardest enterprise threats to expose early. Instead of crude lures and obvious payloads, modern campaigns rely on trusted infrastructure, legitimate-looking authentication f...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: OAuth Trap, EDR Killer, Signal Phishing, Zombie ZIP, AI Platform Hack & More
item.description: Another Thursday, another pile of weird security stuff that somehow happened in just seven days. Some of it is clever. Some of it is lazy. A few bits fall into that uncomfortable category of “yeah… this is probably go...

item.title: CISA Issues Emergency Directive Over Exploited Cisco SD-WAN Flaws
item.description: CISA issued urgent directive as attackers exploit Cisco SD-WAN flaw granting admin access to networks

item.title: Attackers Don't Just Send Phishing Emails. They Weaponize Your SOC's Workload
item.description: The most dangerous phishing campaigns aren’t just designed to fool employees. Many are designed to exhaust the analysts investigating them. When a phishing investigation takes 12 hours instead of five minutes, the out...

item.title: Apple Issues Security Updates for Older iOS Devices Targeted by Coruna WebKit Exploit
item.description: Apple on Wednesday backported fixes for a security flaw in iOS, iPadOS, and macOS Sonoma to older versions after it was found to be used as part of the Coruna exploit kit. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2023-43010,...

item.title: Iran Claims Massive Cyber-Attack on MedTech Firm Stryker
item.description: The pro-Iran Handala group claims to have wiped 200,000 systems in destructive wiper malware attack on US firm Stryker

item.title: Six Android Malware Families Target Pix Payments, Banking Apps, and Crypto Wallets
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered half-a-dozen new Android malware families that come with capabilities to steal data from compromised devices and conduct financial fraud. The Android malware range from tradit...

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited n8n RCE Bug as 24,700 Instances Remain Exposed
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical security flaw impacting n8n to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation...

item.title: Announcing Cloudflare Account Abuse Protection: prevent fraudulent attacks from bots and humans
item.description: Blocking bots isn’t enough anymore. Cloudflare’s new fraud prevention capabilities — now available in Early Access — help stop account abuse before it starts.

item.title: 4 Ways Businesses Use CrowdStrike Charlotte AI to Transform Security Operations
A Guy Who Wrote the Code Died in 2005. I Still Have to Secure It
item.description: The real frontline of American cybersecurity is a bidding war on eBay for 30-year-old industrial controllers.

item.title: INC Ransomware Group Holds Healthcare Hostage in Oceania
item.description: Government agencies, emergency clinics, and others in Australia, New Zealand, and Tonga have had serious run-ins with the prolific ransomware outfit.

item.title: France: National Cybersecurity Agency Reports Ransomware Attack Drop in 2025
item.description: French small and medium businesses remained the organizations most targeted by ransomware in 2025

item.title: Researchers Trick Perplexity's Comet AI Browser Into Phishing Scam in Under Four Minutes
item.description: Agentic web browsers that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities to autonomously execute actions across multiple websites on behalf of a user could be trained and tricked into falling prey to phishing and...

item.title: Infosecurity Europe Announces 2026 Keynote Line Up
item.description: Infosecurity Europe 2026 reveals its keynote line-up, featuring Jason Fox, Shlomo Kramer, Cynthia Kaiser and more, with sessions on AI, cloud security and post quantum threats

item.title: Researchers Uncover ‘LeakyLooker’ Vulnerabilities in Google Looker Studio
item.description: LeakyLooker flaws in Google Looker Studio let attackers run cross-tenant SQL attacks on cloud data

item.description: SAP has released security updates to address two critical security flaws that could be exploited to achieve arbitrary code execution on affected systems. The vulnerabilities in question listed below - CVE-2019-17571 (...

item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2025-68613 n8n Improper Control of Dynamically-Managed Code Resources Vulnerabi...

item.title: What Boards Must Demand in the Age of AI-Automated Exploitation
item.description: “You knew, and you could have acted. Why didn’t you?” This is the question you do not want to be asked. And increasingly, it’s the question leaders are forced to answer after an incident. For years, many executive tea...

item.description: Canada has a choice to make about its artificial intelligence future. The Carney administration is investing $2-billion over five years in its Sovereign AI Compute Strategy . Will any value generated by “sovereign AI”...

item.title: Analyzing "Zombie Zip" Files (CVE-2026-0866), (Wed, Mar 11th)
item.description: A new vulnerability (CVE-2026-0866) has been published: Zombie Zip.

item.title: Five Malicious Rust Crates and AI Bot Exploit CI/CD Pipelines to Steal Developer Secrets
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered five malicious Rust crates that masquerade as time-related utilities to transmit .env file data to the threat actors. The Rust packages, published to crates.io, are listed bel...

item.title: Microsoft Patches 83 CVEs in March Update
item.description: For a change, there's little in this month's Patch Tuesday that should cause panic, according to security experts.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero...

item.description: The RSAC 2026 Conference brings together thousands of professionals, practitioners, vendors, and associations to discuss issues covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity—a place where innovation meets collaboratio...

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday March 2026, (Tue, Mar 10th)
item.description: Microsoft today released patches for 93 vulnerabilities, including 9 vulnerabilities in Chromium affecting Microsoft Edge. 8 of the vulnerabilities are rated critical. 2 were disclosed prior to today but have not yet...

item.title: OpenAI's Promptfoo Deal Plugs Agentic AI Testing Gap
item.description: OpenAI’s latest acquisition addresses a security need Jamieson O’Reilly, security advisor at OpenClaw, raised during an exclusive interview with Infosecurity

item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign where threat actors are abusing FortiGate Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) appliances as entry points to breach victim networks. The activity involves t...

item.title: How to Stop AI Data Leaks: A Webinar Guide to Auditing Modern Agentic Workflows
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer just a tool we talk to; it is a tool that does things for us. These are called AI Agents. They can send emails, move data, and even manage software on their own. But there is...

item.title: KadNap Malware Infects 14,000+ Edge Devices to Power Stealth Proxy Botnet
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new malware called KadNap that's primarily targeting Asus routers to enlist them into a botnet for proxying malicious traffic. The malware, first detected in the wild in Aug...

item.title: Only 24% Of organizations Test Identity Recovery Every Six Months
item.description: Only 24% of organizations test identity disaster recovery plans every 6 months, Quest Software said

item.title: The Zero-Day Scramble is Avoidable: A Guide to Attack Surface Reduction
item.description: You can't control when the next critical vulnerability drops. You can control how much of your environment is exposed when it does. The problem is that most teams have more internet-facing exposure than they realise....

item.title: APT28 Uses BEARDSHELL and COVENANT Malware to Spy on Ukrainian Military
item.description: The Russian state-sponsored hacking group tracked as APT28 has been observed using a pair of implants dubbed BEARDSHELL and COVENANT to facilitate long‑term surveillance of Ukrainian military personnel. The two malwar...

item.title: Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Controls
item.description: Unit 42 research reveals AI judges are vulnerable to stealthy prompt injection. Benign formatting symbols can bypass security controls. The post Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Control...

item.title: ShinyHunters Targets Hundreds of Websites in New Salesforce Campaign
item.description: Prolific ShinyHunters group claims to have stolen data from nearly 400 websites in Experience Cloud attacks

item.title: Are We Ready for Auto Remediation With Agentic AI?
item.description: With the rapid innovations in AI, we are entering an exciting era of automated risk remediation. Learn about security team readiness to leverage agentic AI for threat and exposure management.

item.title: Malicious npm Package Posing as OpenClaw Installer Deploys RAT, Steals macOS Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious npm package that masquerades as an OpenClaw installer to deploy a remote access trojan (RAT) and steal sensitive data from compromised hosts. The package, named "@...

item.title: Threat Actor Exploits Flaws and Uses Elastic Cloud SIEM to Manage Stolen Data
item.description: Huntress researchers uncover campaign exploiting vulnerabilities to steal data using Elastic Cloud as a data hub

item.title: UNC4899 Breached Crypto Firm After Developer AirDropped Trojanized File to Work Device
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as UNC4899 is suspected to be behind a sophisticated cloud compromise campaign targeting a cryptocurrency organization in 2025 to steal millions of dollars in cryptocurrency. The ac...

item.title: Active defense: introducing a stateful vulnerability scanner for APIs
item.description: Cloudflare’s new Web and API Vulnerability Scanner helps teams proactively find logic flaws. By using AI to build API call graphs, we identify vulnerabilities that standard defensive tools miss.

item.title: UK Launches New Crackdown Unit to Tackle Cyber-Fraud at the Source
item.description: New UK Online Crime Centre will combine expertise from a range of sources to takedown online channels cyber-scammers rely on

item.title: AI Security Startups Dominate New Cyber Innovation Awards
item.description: Over one in five winners of IT-Harvest’s 2026 Cyber 150 are AI security companies

item.title: Chinese Cyber Threat Lurks In Critical Asian Sectors for Years
item.description: An undefined Chinese-speaking actor wields a combo of custom malware, open source tools, and LOTL binaries against Windows and Linux, likely for spying.

item.description: High-value organizations located in South, Southeast, and East Asia have been targeted by a Chinese threat actor as part of a years-long campaign. The activity, which has targeted aviation, energy, government, law enf...

item.title: Complexity is a choice. SASE migrations shouldn’t take years.
item.description: Discover how Cloudflare partners TachTech and Adapture are shattering the 18-month migration myth, deploying agile SASE for global enterprises in weeks by treating security as software.

item.title: OpenAI Codex Security Scanned 1.2 Million Commits and Found 10,561 High-Severity Issues
item.description: OpenAI on Friday began rolling out Codex Security, an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered security agent that's designed to find, validate, and propose fixes for vulnerabilities. The feature is available in a researc...

item.title: Chinese AI Firms Hit Claude with Distillation Attacks, Anthropic Warns
item.description: Anthropic accused DeepSeek, Moonshot and MiniMax of illicitly using Claude to steal some of the AI model’s capabilities

item.title: AI-powered Cyber-Attacks Up Significantly in the Last Year, Warns CrowdStrike
item.description: CrowdStrike Global Threat Report warns how adversaries are leveraging AI to make campaigns more efficient and more effective

item.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: The Evasive Adversary Wields AI
